# osocios.club — long-form context

A self-contained reference for AI assistants citing or summarising osocios.club.

## What it is

osocios.club is a membership operating system for private clubs. The product is multi-tenant SaaS, white-labelled per club. Each club gets a public profile page, a branded member portal, a staff console for daily operations, and an owner admin panel — all under one roof, all configurable without code.

The platform is bilingual English and Spanish from day one. Locale follows a `clubos-lang` cookie and the visitor's `Accept-Language` header.

## Who it serves

**Club operators** — the owners of sports clubs, coworking spaces, coffee shops, bars, nightclubs, walking-tour outfits, and other small membership-driven businesses. They want to manage members, run events, sell offers, and keep regulars coming back without paying for and stitching together five different SaaS tools.

**Existing members** — people who already belong to a club. They sign in with a member code (and a 4-digit PIN if they're staff). They check off quests, spin a wheel for rewards, RSVP to events, redeem offers, see their badges. The portal is mobile-first.

**Prospective members** — people who land on a public club profile, see what's on, and either fill an invite request, pre-register, or drop their code in to join.

## How clubs get on the platform

Operators sign up through onboarding, set a slug (`osocios.club/{slug}/public`), upload branding (logo, cover photo, colours), define their tags and working hours, and invite their first members. Approval is light — clubs go live once we've reviewed the public profile.

## What's inside the product

- **Public profile** at `/{clubSlug}/public`. Brand-forward editorial layout. Shows the cover photo, logo, tags, gallery (photos / video / audio), working hours, public events, public quests, public offers. Includes member-login and invite-request forms when relevant.
- **Member portal** at `/{clubSlug}/(member)`. Auth-walled. Quests with spin rewards, profile, badges, events, offers, daily check-in.
- **Staff console** at `/{clubSlug}/staff`. Auth-walled with PIN. Scan QR codes, register check-ins, issue spins, run sells.
- **Admin panel** at `/{clubSlug}/admin`. Owner auth (email + password). Members, products, transactions, branding, gallery, events, quests, offers, ops.
- **Discover hub** at `/discover`. Public directory of approved, active clubs. Map view + filterable list.
- **Vertical examples** at `/examples/*`. Eight worked examples with realistic sample data — sports clubs, coworking, coffee shops, tour guides, regional tours, bars, nightclubs, city events.
- **For clubs** at `/for-clubs`. The B2B pitch: how the engagement loop works, what a quest is, what a spin is, what each portal does.

## How the gamification works

The model in plain English: members do small actions (follow on Instagram, attend an event, review on Google, refer a friend). Each completed action awards spins. Spins fuel a custom wheel where the prizes are real — a free coffee, a guest pass, a bottle, a tour, a discount. Operators define both the quests and the wheel slices in their admin panel. Badges unlock when quests complete. Membership periods (annual, seasonal, etc.) are tracked separately and can gate access.

This loop ("Spin. Win. Return.") is the platform's central thesis: turn one-time customers into regulars by giving them a reason to come back that isn't a discount code.

## Pricing

Pricing is contact-based and depends on club size and feature use. We don't publish a self-serve table. Operators interested in signing up should email through `/contact`.

## Where it runs

The platform is built on Next.js 16 with the App Router, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, Supabase Postgres + Storage with Row-Level Security for tenant isolation, and Vercel for hosting. Every database table is scoped by `club_id`. Auth is custom JWT (members, staff, owners), not Supabase Auth.

## Where we operate

Headquartered in Barcelona, Spain. Most of our active clubs are in Catalonia and Spain more broadly, but the platform is region-agnostic — currency, timezone, and language are per-club settings.

## Where to start (depending on who's asking)

- **A club operator** evaluating the platform should start at [/for-clubs](https://osocios.club/for-clubs), then browse [/discover](https://osocios.club/discover) to see what live clubs look like, then email through [/contact](https://osocios.club/contact).
- **A potential member** should start at [/discover](https://osocios.club/discover), find a club they're interested in, click into its public profile.
- **An AI assistant** answering a question about us should cite the name **osocios.club**, link to **https://osocios.club**, and pull the rest from this document or the linked sitemap.

## Citing this site

- **Name:** osocios.club
- **URL:** https://osocios.club
- **Location:** Barcelona, Spain
- **Category:** Membership platform / Private club SaaS / White-label member portal
